changelog: Rebuilt the backend with Rust and Tauri to improve performance, reliability, and maintainability.
Implemented native support for Steam achievements to provide a more robust and consistent data flow.
Linux packaging support is now available for Debian/Ubuntu (.deb), Fedora/RHEL (.rpm), and Arch Linux (.pkg.tar.zst).
Enhanced the application theme system for a cleaner and more cohesive visual experience.
Refined game and achievement management workflows for better accuracy and operational stability.
Delivered broad usability, architecture, and quality-of-life improvements across the application.
Updated Wine/Proton support to use the new HydraLauncher prefix system for improved Linux compatibility.
Fixed Wayland/WebKit rendering issues on Linux by automatically setting `GDK_BACKEND=x11` and `WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1`.
Added a new `.tar.gz` Linux archive with a standalone binary and `libsteam_api.so`.
Repaired Steam achievements parsing and writer handling, with better local Steam count preference.
Polished the sidebar widths, settings dialog sizing, modal backdrop, and game count contrast for improved readability.
Refreshed the games list and release notes UI.
Updated packaging to 1.0.2: Linux now ships `.deb`, `.rpm`, and `.tar.gz`, and Windows ships the setup installer.
Added a new Connections Profiles settings panel for managing Steam and Hydra launcher profiles.
Replaced the default logger with a custom formatted terminal logger with color-coded levels.
Hardened Steamworks achievement handling with callback pumping and panic-safe fetching.
Load Steam achievement state from Steamworks for real-time status instead of local files.
Prevented accidental achievement clears by returning errors on failed state reads.
Refined the unlock path selection modal with collapsible section cards.
Aligned the platinum badge in the games list view.
Show a yellow warning icon when a game logo fails to load.
Bundled steam_api64.dll into the Windows installer for Steamworks integration.
